macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Futures Decline Amid Middle East Conflict Renewal
items.summary: Dow futures fell 0.09% to 46,480, S&P futures slipped 0.03% to 6,632.75, and Nasdaq futures dropped 0.08% as stocks declined following Monday's recovery due to renewed Middle East tensions.

items.title: US Stocks Swing Between Losses and Gains on Iran War Uncertainty
items.summary: US stock market oscillated between losses and gains on Tuesday amid ongoing uncertainty about the duration of the war with Iran.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Ripple Prime Launches XRP as 24/7 Institutional Collateral
items.summary: XRP has transitioned from a retail ticker to institutional collateral backing CME trades, with Ripple Prime implementing infrastructure to bypass liquidation traps affecting institutional funds. The platform expects a $120 billion influx if pending ETFs receive approval by March 27th.

items.title: SWIFT Confirms 25+ Banks Going Live with Crypto for Cross-Border Payments by June
items.summary: The global payments network SWIFT has confirmed that over 25 banks will launch cryptocurrency-based 24/7 cross-border payment capabilities by June 2026, marking major institutional adoption.

items.title: BlackRock Expands Crypto Portfolio with 11 Coins
items.summary: BlackRock has significantly increased its cryptocurrency exposure across 11 digital assets, signaling major institutional capital inflow into the crypto market.

items.title: Cryptocurrency Markets Rally Following Iran Tensions De-escalation
items.summary: Bitcoin and major altcoins experienced significant gains as Donald Trump delayed his ultimatum regarding Iran following diplomatic discussions, triggering short squeezes across markets.

items.title: Major Price Movements: Bitcoin +29%, Ethereum +38%, XRP +16%
items.summary: Cryptocurrency markets showed strong bullish momentum with Bitcoin gaining 29%, Ethereum up 38%, and XRP rising 16% amid institutional adoption news and geopolitical developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Darwin Gödel Machine (DGM-H) Breakthrough: AI Systems Achieve Recursive Self-Improvement
items.summary: Researchers documented the Darwin Gödel Machine and its extension DGM-Hyperagents, a self-referential system that iteratively modifies its own code and validates changes against empirical benchmarks. The framework is now publicly accessible for developers to create self-improving coding pipelines.

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.4 with Efficient Frontier Models
items.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5.4 alongside GPT-5.4 Mini and Nano variants, marking a pivotal moment in frontier model efficiency and deployment capabilities across different computational scales.

items.title: Meta Announces Custom AI Chips MTIA 300-500 to Reduce NVIDIA Dependence
items.summary: Meta unveiled four new custom AI chips designed to reduce reliance on NVIDIA and optimize content ranking and recommendations across its data centers by 2027.

items.title: Mamba 3 Open-Source Framework Challenges Transformer Architecture
items.summary: Mamba 3, an open-source framework released on March 17, offers enhancements to improve efficiency and performance in machine learning tasks, potentially reshaping future AI applications beyond traditional transformer architectures.

items.title: MIT Develops 'Humble' AI Framework for Medical Diagnostics
items.summary: An MIT-led international team created a framework enabling AI systems to reveal uncertainty in medical diagnoses and recommendations, including an Epistemic Virtue Score module that ensures confidence is appropriately tempered by clinical complexity.

items.title: Assam, India Launches AI-First Governance Initiative
items.summary: The state of Assam is embedding agentic AI into disaster preparedness through the Assam State Data Policy 2026 to predict flood impacts on specific infrastructure in real-time.

items.title: Larus Awarded $8.3M Contract for AI-Powered Tactical Intelligence
items.summary: Larus received an $8.3 million IDEaS Test Drive contract from Canada's Department of National Defence to advance AI-powered tactical intelligence capabilities.

items.title: AI Skills Become Non-Negotiable for Tech Roles in 2026
items.summary: 71% of tech leaders now consider AI and machine learning skills essential for developers—exceeding cloud computing skills requirements—as companies increasingly hire AI specialists across departments.

items.title: Global AI Market Surges Past $40B in U.S. with Explosive Growth Projections
items.summary: U.S. AI markets have exceeded $40 billion with projections reaching hundreds of billions worldwide as enterprises accelerate adoption of intelligent solutions across multiple industries.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Epic Games Lays Off 1,000 Employees Amid Fortnite Downturn
items.summary: Epic Games announced layoffs of 1,000 workers due to falling player interest in Fortnite and spending exceeding revenue, aiming for over $500 million in cost savings.

items.title: Epic Games Cuts 1,000 Jobs as Costs Outpace Revenue
items.summary: Cary-based Epic Games laid off 1,000 employees, citing Fortnite's declining performance and operational overspending exceeding $500 million in targeted savings.

items.title: Nintendo Slashes Switch 2 Production by a Third Due to Weak U.S. Sales
items.summary: Nintendo is reportedly reducing Switch 2 output significantly amid dwindling demand in the United States.

items.title: Rockstar Games Requires Developers to Sign Lifetime NDAs
items.summary: Rockstar Games developers are signing lifetime non-disclosure agreements, raising concerns about long-term secrecy obligations.

items.title: Screen Australia Invests $1.4 Million in 26 Local Game Projects
items.summary: Screen Australia allocated $1.4 million to 26 Australian video game projects through its Games Production and Emerging Gamemakers funds, supporting local stories and diverse creators.

items.title: Tencent Showcases GenAI Animation Tools at GDC 2026
items.summary: Tencent demonstrated generative AI tools for animations, voices, and NPCs at the GDC Festival of Gaming, though questions remain on enhancing game fun despite technical impressiveness.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Musk's SpaceX and Tesla Undertake Terafab Project for Orbital Data Centers
items.summary: SpaceX is partnering with Tesla on an unprecedented Terafab initiative to deploy data center satellites in orbit, featuring massive solar panels to harness the sun's energy 24/7 for computing operations both on Earth and in space.

items.title: Pentagon's Project Maven Becomes Official Program of Record
items.summary: The Pentagon has officially designated Maven, an artificial intelligence system, as a program of record, accelerating AI-enabled decision making for linking sensors and shooters in combat operations.

items.title: Micron Technology Shares Decline Amid CapEx Concerns
items.summary: Micron shares fell 4.4% and are down for a third consecutive day following the company's announcement of higher-than-expected capital expenditure for the year.

items.title: Xiaomi Reports Slowest Growth Since 2023 Amid Memory Chip Shortage
items.summary: Xiaomi is expecting less than 7% revenue growth, a significant decline from 20-30% growth in previous quarters, driven by memory chip shortages that are pressuring margins in its competitive smartphone segment.
